items.situation: two friends deciding where to get lunch
items.speaker: Do you feel like grabbing a bite at the new café?

options.A: Sure, I'm always up for that.
options.B: Their sandwiches are named after cities.
options.C: She moved into the dorms last week.
options.D: It's right across from the library.

items.explanation: 正确答案 A 用口语惯用表达 "I'm always up for that"（我一向乐意）自然地接受了午餐邀请，符合 idiomatic 范式。B 只是在描述这家咖啡馆（三明治用城市命名），属于语义联想陷阱，没有回应邀请。C 讲的是另一个人搬进宿舍，与话题完全无关，是离题干扰项。D 回答的是咖啡馆的位置，而说话人问的是"你想不想去"，答非所问。

items.situation: coordinating airport pickup with a friend
items.speaker: My flight lands around six tomorrow evening.

options.A: The plane has really comfortable seats.
options.B: Okay, I'll be waiting at arrivals.
options.C: I finished painting the kitchen today.
options.D: Sure, dinner at six sounds great.

items.explanation: 正确答案 B 以话语标记 "Okay" 开头，间接承诺去接机（在到达口等你），符合 marker_led_indirect 范式，直接解决了接机的需求。A 只是描述飞机座位舒适，是从 flight/plane 联想出的语义陷阱。C 说自己刷了厨房，与接机完全无关，是离题干扰项。D 把 "six"（六点降落）误解成六点吃晚饭的邀约，属于误解陷阱。

items.situation: student checking with TA about lab report requirements
items.speaker: I'm not sure what the lab report needs.

options.A: The lab has brand-new microscopes now.
options.B: I turned in my essay this morning.
options.C: Well, it just needs your methods and results.
options.D: Yes, the lab is open until nine.

items.explanation: 正确答案 C 以话语标记 "Well" 开头，间接而明确地告诉学生报告只需包含方法和结果，符合 marker_led_indirect 范式。A 描述实验室新添了显微镜，是从 lab 联想出的语义陷阱，没回答内容要求。B 说自己交了论文，与实验报告的要求无关，是离题干扰项。D 回答的是实验室开放到几点，答的是错误的方面（开放时间而非报告内容）。

items.speaker: Why can't we just keep the original appointment?

options.A: The office has moved to a new building.
options.B: I planted some tomatoes over the weekend.
options.C: It's scheduled for ten in the morning.
options.D: Well, the doctor's out of town that day.

items.explanation: 正确答案 D 以话语标记 "Well" 开头，间接给出无法保留原预约的原因（医生那天出差），符合 marker_led_indirect 范式，正面回应了"为什么"。A 描述办公室搬了新楼，是从 appointment/office 联想出的语义陷阱，没解释原因。B 说自己周末种了番茄，完全离题。C 回答的是预约在上午十点（时间），而说话人问的是"为什么"，答非所问。

items.situation: expressing concern about a friend's housing situation
items.speaker: Where are you staying since the move fell through?

options.A: Honestly, I've landed on my feet.
options.B: The moving boxes are still in the hallway.
options.C: I caught a great movie last night.
options.D: I'm staying for the whole show.

items.explanation: 正确答案 A 用惯用语 "landed on my feet"（安顿得很好、化险为夷）来宽慰对方，回应了朋友的关心，符合 idiomatic 范式。B 描述搬家纸箱还在走廊，是从 move 联想出的语义陷阱，没回答现在住哪。C 说昨晚看了场好电影，完全离题。D 把 "staying"（住在哪里）误用成"留下来看完整场演出"，是一词多义/误解陷阱，尤其容易迷惑。

items.situation: student asking about a dorm maintenance issue
items.speaker: Did you report the broken heater to maintenance yet?

options.A: The heater is a pretty old model.
options.B: Should I call them, or will you?
options.C: I signed up for the cooking class.
options.D: It's down in the basement somewhere.

items.explanation: 正确答案 B 用一个反问 "Should I call them, or will you?" 推进对话、厘清由谁去报修，符合 counter_question 范式。A 描述暖气是个旧型号，是从 heater 联想出的语义陷阱，没回应是否报修。C 说自己报了烹饪课，完全离题。D 回答的是暖气/维修室在地下室某处（地点），而问题是"报修了没有"，答非所问。

items.situation: student asking a professor about office hours
items.speaker: What's the best time to catch you this week?

options.A: My office just got a fresh coat of paint.
options.B: The lecture ran a bit long today.
options.C: My door's always open in the afternoons.
options.D: It's on the third floor of Hale.

items.explanation: 正确答案 C 用惯用表达 "My door's always open"（我随时欢迎你来）间接告诉学生下午都可以，符合 idiomatic 范式。A 描述办公室刚粉刷过，是从 office 联想出的语义陷阱，没回答时间。B 说今天讲课拖堂了，与"什么时候方便"无关，属离题。D 回答的是办公室在 Hale 楼三层（地点），而学生问的是"最佳时间"，答非所问。

items.situation: friend sharing news about a job interview
items.speaker: How did the whole interview thing turn out?

options.A: The questions were printed on blue cards.
options.B: I repainted my bike over the weekend.
options.C: It was over at the downtown office.
options.D: Actually, they offered me the position.

items.explanation: 正确答案 D 以话语标记 "Actually" 开头，间接分享了面试结果（对方给了 offer），符合 marker_led_indirect 范式，正面回应了"怎么样"。A 描述面试题印在蓝卡上，是从 interview 联想出的语义陷阱，没说结果。B 说自己周末给自行车重新喷漆了，完全离题。C 回答的是面试在市中心办公室举行（地点），而问题是"结果如何"，答非所问。
